what_i_did: Performed design-only direction review for Issue #15 / P2-12. No scans, target interaction, module execution, scanner invocation, network/callback behavior, or repository modification were performed by the reviewer.

references.useful_pattern: Stable diagnostic rule IDs, structured result metadata, and precise locations.
references.safety_concern: Do not expand into code-scanning upload/report workflows in this phase.
references.contract_impact: Use stable bundle codes and path-specific diagnostics.

references.useful_pattern: Errors carry instance paths, schema paths, validator names, and contextual details.
references.safety_concern: Keep current stdlib-only validators; do not add framework dependency for this issue.
references.contract_impact: Add structured error_details with document/key paths, expected, observed.

references.useful_pattern: Static policy evaluation over local documents, separated from action/execution.
references.safety_concern: Do not introduce policy engine dependency or live target checks.
references.contract_impact: Keep bundle validator deterministic, local, non-executing, and fail-closed.

references.useful_pattern: Versioned structured documents and compatibility-oriented validation.
references.safety_concern: Do not turn the module bundle into a full SBOM model.
references.contract_impact: Preserve versioned bundle contract discipline and update-friendly diagnostics.

references.useful_pattern: Useful template IDs and metadata conventions.
references.adopt_adapt_ignore: ignore execution / adapt metadata lessons only
references.safety_concern: Do not copy active scanning/template execution behavior.
references.contract_impact: Use as a negative boundary reminder: validation remains offline-only.

adopted_concepts: Stable explicit bundle diagnostic codes instead of free-form-only errors.
Structured detail objects with code, path, message, expected, observed, and module_id where useful.
Cross-document consistency checks should identify exact diverging keys.
Tests should assert codes and selected detail fields, not only broad denial.
rejected_unsafe_concepts: No module execution to verify I/O bundles.
No persisted preview ledger in this phase.
No automatic normalization that hides mismatches that should fail closed.
implementation_guidance: Add structured error_details while preserving existing errors/error_codes fields.
Add BUNDLE_CREATED_AT_UTC_MISMATCH for run.created_at_utc vs module_input.run.created_at_utc.
Add explicit tests for all P2-12 requested BUNDLE_* codes.
Use small helpers to compare values and report paths.
Defer profile-driven expected constraint tables to a later phase unless needed for current tests.
safety_boundary: Offline helper/test/documentation work only. No live scans, target interaction, real module execution, scanner execution, subprocess launch for module behavior, network clients, sockets/DNS, callbacks, exploit/fuzz/brute-force behavior, persisted preview audit ledger, real findings/evidence, config/scope.txt changes, credentials, loot, generated reports, scheduler, deployment, billing, or production settings.
architecture_fit: Fits the long-term systematized, modular, update-friendly authorized-testing platform by improving offline diagnostics and contract robustness before any executor or ledger work.
